headline: Amazon buys Fauna Robotics, maker of the Sprout humanoid robot
summary: Amazon has acquired Fauna Robotics, just under two months after the startup introduced a humanoid robot called Sprout designed to be a friendly addition to social spaces like homes and schools.  The e-commerce giant is already a robotics powerhouse, having boasted of deploying more than 1 million robots across its warehouse operations, but bringing the 1.5-foot-tall, rectangular-headed Sprout on board adds a robot that's more about fun interactions than heavy lifting.

headline: EU antitrust chief meets Google, Meta, OpenAI, Amazon CEOs amidst AI scrutiny
summary: EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera, who has ‌voiced concerns about Big Tech extending ‌its dominance into artificial intelligence, will meet the CEOs of ​Alphabet, Meta Platforms and OpenAI on Tuesday, a European Commission agenda item shows. The first-time meetings with Sundar Pichai, Mark Zuckerberg, and Sam Altman ‌in San Francisco ⁠will take place as part of Ribera's week-long trip to the United ⁠States where she is scheduled to speak at an American Bar Association conference on Friday. She will ​also meet ​Amazon CEO Andy ​Jassy on Wednesday.

headline: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
summary: Zoox, Amazon's self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi ‌service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, ‌and begin testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking ​its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing market.  Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.  Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.

raw.headline: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
raw.summary: Zoox, Amazon's self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi ‌service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, ‌and begin testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking ​its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing market.  Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.  Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.

headline: Inside Arm’s $71 million chip lab where its making its first ever CPU
summary: For more than 35 years, Arm has licensed its chip architecture and collected royalties on every processor made by customers like Apple, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, Intel and Microsoft. Now Arm is becoming their competitor, making physical silicon of its own for the first time, with Meta as the initial customer. CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

raw.headline: Inside Arm’s $71 million chip lab where its making its first ever CPU
raw.summary: For more than 35 years, Arm has licensed its chip architecture and collected royalties on every processor made by customers like Apple, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, Intel and Microsoft. Now Arm is becoming their competitor, making physical silicon of its own for the first time, with Meta as the initial customer. CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

headline: Tradr Launches Leveraged ETFs on AMZN, AAOI, HL and IBM
summary: Tradr ETFs, a provider of ETFs designed for sophisticated investors and professional traders, today launched four new leveraged ETFs on single stocks. The Cboe-listed funds seek to deliver either two times the inverse (-200%) or two times long (200%) the daily performance of a specific underlying stock. All four ETFs are first-to-market strategies.

All four ETFs are first-to-market strategies.

headline: Tech Weekly: Amazon's smartphone comeback, OpenAI's big offer
summary: STORY: From a potential smartphone comeback for Amazon to OpenAI's big offer... This is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyAmazon plans a smartphone comeback, more than a decade after one of the firm's most high-profile flops.The Fire Phone came out in 2014, but was cancelled just over a year later.Sources said Amazon's latest smartphone effort is known internally as 'Transformer'.The timeline for the project is unclear, and the sources warned it could be ditched if the strategy shifts or due to financial concerns.Sources said OpenAI wants to form joint ventures with buyout firms.They added the ChatGPT maker has offered private-equity firms a guaranteed minimum return of 17.5%, and early access to its newest AI models as part of the move.It aims to raise fresh capital and push forward adoption of enterprise AI products. The sources further said OpenAI is trying to offer a more attractive deal than rival Anthropic, which is also looking for partnerships.Rivian struck a deal with Uber to provide robotaxis to the ride-hailing service.Both companies said Uber will invest up to $1.25 billion into the electric vehicle maker.In turn, Rivian will provide 10,000 fully autonomous robotaxis starting in two years' time.Investors liked what they heard as Rivian shares jumped as much as a tenth on the update.The Amazon Web Services region in Bahrain was "disrupted" amid the current conflict in the Middle East.It marked the second time in a month its operations were affected by the war.Amazon said the disruption was due to drone activity in the area.The company said it's helping to migrate customers to alternate AWS regions while it recovers.Although it didn't provide more details on the extent of the damage or how long it believes the disruption could last.SpaceX and Tesla will build two advanced chip factories at a facility in Austin, Texas.That was the word from CEO Elon Musk, who unveiled the 'Terafab' project.He said one facility will make chips for cars and humanoid robots.While Musk said the other is due to make processors for AI data centers in space.

raw.headline: Tech Weekly: Amazon's smartphone comeback, OpenAI's big offer
raw.summary: STORY: From a potential smartphone comeback for Amazon to OpenAI's big offer... This is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyAmazon plans a smartphone comeback, more than a decade after one of the firm's most high-profile flops.The Fire Phone came out in 2014, but was cancelled just over a year later.Sources said Amazon's latest smartphone effort is known internally as 'Transformer'.The timeline for the project is unclear, and the sources warned it could be ditched if the strategy shifts or due to financial concerns.Sources said OpenAI wants to form joint ventures with buyout firms.They added the ChatGPT maker has offered private-equity firms a guaranteed minimum return of 17.5%, and early access to its newest AI models as part of the move.It aims to raise fresh capital and push forward adoption of enterprise AI products. The sources further said OpenAI is trying to offer a more attractive deal than rival Anthropic, which is also looking for partnerships.Rivian struck a deal with Uber to provide robotaxis to the ride-hailing service.Both companies said Uber will invest up to $1.25 billion into the electric vehicle maker.In turn, Rivian will provide 10,000 fully autonomous robotaxis starting in two years' time.Investors liked what they heard as Rivian shares jumped as much as a tenth on the update.The Amazon Web Services region in Bahrain was "disrupted" amid the current conflict in the Middle East.It marked the second time in a month its operations were affected by the war.Amazon said the disruption was due to drone activity in the area.The company said it's helping to migrate customers to alternate AWS regions while it recovers.Although it didn't provide more details on the extent of the damage or how long it believes the disruption could last.SpaceX and Tesla will build two advanced chip factories at a facility in Austin, Texas.That was the word from CEO Elon Musk, who unveiled the 'Terafab' project.He said one facility will make chips for cars and humanoid robots.While Musk said the other is due to make processors for AI data centers in space.

headline: Amazon Is Ready to Take on StarLink in Space-Based Broadband
summary: Starlink has turned space-based internet into a major business for SpaceX. What began as prototypes around 2019 has grown into a constellation of more than 11,500 launched satellites, with over 10,000 actively in orbit by early 2026. Subscriber numbers have also climbed steadily: from about 2.3 million at the end of 2023 to roughly 4.6 ... Amazon Is Ready to Take on StarLink in Space-Based Broadband

headline: Iran War Disrupts Amazon Data Centers in Bahrain
summary: Amazon Web Services said the conflict in the Middle East has disrupted its cluster of data centers in Bahrain.
